summary: PET/CT has limited utility compared to MDCT because it lacks sufficient spatial resolution and sensitivity for reliably identifying small hepatic metastases; while helpful in specific cases like differentiating recurrence from inflammation, its role in routine preoperative staging is minimal. Thus, MDCT remains preferred as first-line imaging due to higher diagnostic accuracy and practicality.
